body.title: [sprint] Intent extractor: DRAFTS and VALIDATES intentions — it never WRITES the intention field. Direction is the whole safety property.
body.intention: I intend that the intent extractor may derive an intention from any evidence CAUSALLY UPSTREAM of the implementation — the ticket description, the decision, the conversation that produced it — and may WRITE it, tagged with its provenance; and that it may NEVER derive an intention from the artifact that intention is meant to falsify, because a spec taken from the code cannot judge the code it came from.
body.description:
**⚠ RE-SCOPED 2026-07-13 — THE BLANKET BAN IS REPEALED (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13; Akien: 'i agree with fable').**

**WHAT CC GOT WRONG (twice, in two days).** On 2026-07-12 CC forbade this device from ever writing an `intention`, on a circularity argument: the property mutation-red breaks IS the intention, so an intention inferred from the implementation is tested against the implementation it came from and passes on ANY build, including a hollow one.

The KERNEL is sound and survives. The SCOPE was wrong, on three counts:
  1. **'It passes on ANY implementation' is FALSE.** It passes on the CURRENT one and any future one preserving the property — it goes RED on future DIVERGENCE. Two threats were conflated: (a) is TODAY's code hollow (an extracted intention genuinely cannot tell you) and (b) did TOMORROW's code drift (it tells you perfectly well). An extracted intention is a RATCHET, not a VERDICT. For 150 sprint tickets whose code DOES NOT EXIST YET, threat (a) is not even live.
  2. **THIS DEVICE NEVER READ THE CODE.** MEASURED: it reads the TICKET DESCRIPTION (cc_queue.py:1519-1545) — written BEFORE any code exists. That is UPSTREAM evidence. The circle requires deriving the spec from the artifact the spec will judge; this device does not close that circle and never did. CC banned the one case that was cleanly legitimate.
  3. **THE SAFETY PROPERTY IS NOT AUTHORSHIP.** A ratified string and a machine-written string are BYTE-IDENTICAL; authority cannot live in the typing. It lives in WHAT EVIDENCE CONDITIONED IT. And ratification by a human who does not remember the ticket is a RITUAL — that human is inferring from the artifact too, only slower and more confidently.

**ALSO MEASURED — the safety property CC 'ruled' was ALREADY THE CODE.** The device writes `inferred_intention`, NEVER `intention`. 1,735 tickets carry one. CC re-derived an existing invariant and presented it as a new constraint.

**THE CORPUS IS HEALTHY AND IT IS THE GOOD NEWS.** 1,722 joinable training pairs in devlab.validations: `context = the ticket description Akien wrote`, `outcome = the intention Akien wrote`. Harvested automatically from every ticket filed WITH an intention. This is a real supervised loop with human ground truth, and it has been quietly accumulating for a month.

**THE NARROW BAN (this is the whole rule):** AN INTENTION MAY NOT BE DERIVED SOLELY FROM THE ARTIFACT IT IS MEANT TO FALSIFY.

**WHAT THIS DEVICE DOES:**
  1. **EXTRACT (upstream)** — derive an intention from description / decision / conversation. WRITE it, tagged `provenance_class: extracted-upstream`. This is a RATCHET: it guards drift, it does not certify the build. It may be PROMOTED to `declared` when a human with independent knowledge confirms it.
  2. **VALIDATE** — compare a DECLARED intention against the code and FLAG divergence. Never overwrite. This composes with the intention_date check: THE DATE FLAGS, THE EXTRACTOR ADJUDICATES.
  3. **FEED** the scenario generator.

**FORBIDDEN:** writing an intention derived from the implementation and tagging it `declared`, or letting an `extracted-from-artifact` intention gate a proof-on-close. Only `declared` is a SPEC.

**BLOCKED ON:** T-intent-extractor-crash-masquerades-as-refusal — the device is ~99% broken in production (2,436/2,470 predictions are the `except` fallback). It has 1,722 examples to learn from and cannot emit a single word. Fix the parse first; the drafts for the 150 intentionless sprint tickets then come essentially for free, in Akien's own voice, learned from his own 1,722 prior intentions.

**Proof obligation:** a hollow build tags everything `extracted-upstream` and calls it done. What it could NOT pass: a fixture where an intention derived from the IMPLEMENTATION is offered as a proof-on-close spec and is REJECTED at the gate; and one where an `extracted-upstream` intention correctly goes RED when the code later diverges (proving the ratchet actually ratchets).
body.completion_criteria: Machine never writes `intention` (proven by an unratified-draft fixture). A contradicting declared intention raises a FLAG and overwrites nothing (the discriminating fixture — a 'plausible sentence' build fails it). Drafts land in a human ratification queue. The 150 live sprint tickets lacking an intention are surfaced as a worklist.
